title: The Symbol, Sacredness, and the Sacred
essence: Drawing together symbol, sacredness, and ultimate reality, Vervaeke explores how symbolic engagement implements anagogic transformation. Symbols enable 'holding in mind' (e.g., the scales for justice) through metaphorical exaptation, allowing bi-directional activation of embodied cognitive machinery. Meditation on breath or flag patriotism exemplifies symbolic resonance: participatory integration alternates with disclosure, each deepening the other without stabilizing on fixed meaning. Moving from experiential sacredness to metaphysical claims about the Sacred, he argues for inexhaustibility rather than supernatural essence. Reality and the self are both ultimately inexhaustible no-thingness (emptiness), and sacredness emerges from their deep transjective identity. The sacred is not an object with absolute value but the ongoing co-emergence of meaning-making process with reality itself—what Plato has permanently been for him: an inexhaustible source of gnosis requiring constant return and re-exaptation.
keyClaims: Symbols enact anagogic resonance: as we become more integrated, we see more deeply; seeing more deeply enables deeper integration.
Symbols work through exaptation: backward-reaching activation of embodied machinery combined with forward-reaching openness to emergence.
The sacred is not metaphysically supernatural but inexhaustibly open-ended; sacredness emerges from inexhaustible coupling of reality and relevance realization.
Mythos serves religio by activating, accentuating, articulating the machinery of meaning-making itself.
What makes something sacred is not objective essence but inexhaustible capacity to transform self and disclose world reciprocally.

concepts.name: anagoge
concepts.summary: Spiral of deepening integration and world-disclosure; symbol-mediated transformation of both self and world.

concepts.name: holding in mind
concepts.summary: Using symbols to contemplate abstract concepts through embodied-participatory engagement rather than propositional definition.

concepts.name: exaptation
concepts.summary: Bi-directional use of embodied circuits: activating them through symbol, then using their activation for abstract understanding.

concepts.name: symbol resonance
concepts.summary: Oscillating between participatory integration and perspectival disclosure; each pole strengthens the other.

concepts.name: mythos
concepts.summary: Symbol + story + ritual together; enactive mythology for activating religio through serious play.

concepts.name: religio
concepts.summary: Binding-together of self and world; the machinery of meaning-making; what mythos activates and celebrates.

concepts.name: gnosis
concepts.summary: Direct intuitive knowing through repeated return and deepening engagement; ongoing transformation without final form.

concepts.name: infinite regress problem
concepts.summary: The I can never see itself; framing can never frame itself; creates phenomenological mystery at core of consciousness.

concepts.name: transjective
concepts.summary: The inexhaustibility of reality meets the inexhaustibility of relevance realization in co-emergent fashion.

people.name: Michael Polanyi
people.summary: On subsidiary and focal awareness; on indwelling; on holding things in mind through participatory engagement.

people.name: Vilayanur Ramachandran
people.summary: Buba-Kiki experiments showing synesthetic symbol perception; participatory nature of symbol engagement.

people.name: James
people.summary: On I-me distinction; provides philosophical grounding for participatory symbol theory.

people.name: Spinoza
people.summary: Ethics as extended meditation for scientia intuitiva; symbols as means to cosmic perspective.

people.name: D.T. Suzuki
people.summary: On prajña and non-duality; scaling up and down simultaneously; perspective interpenetration.

people.name: Meister Eckhart
people.summary: Neoplatonic Christian mystic; 'the eye by which I see God is the eye by which God sees me.'

people.name: Plato
people.summary: Exemplar of sacred inexhaustibility for Vervaeke; endless return and deepening without final form.

people.name: Nietzsche
people.summary: On staring into abyss; transgressive aspect of sacredness.

crossRefs.context: Previous episode on symbol and music; this deepens the theoretical framework.
Earlier episode on sacredness; this develops metaphysical implications.
Next episode addresses perennial problems and reverse engineering enlightenment using this machinery.

quotableMoments.quote: Symbols have the capacity to afford Anagoge. They afford a capacity whereby I get inner-optimization and then I see more deeply into the thing, and then by seeing more deeply, I get more inner optimization and the whole process flows Anagogically.
The sacred is the Transjective relationship between the combinatorially explosive nature of Reality and the inexhaustible process of Relevance Realization.
quotableMoments.context: Central thesis on sacred as inexhaustible coupling rather than metaphysical object.

quotableMoments.quote: I can return again and again to Plato and see what I did not see before... there isn't some final form there! There is an inexhaustible fount of insight, transformation, an inexhaustible source of trans-framing.
quotableMoments.context: Practical exemplification of how sacredness works through endless return and deepening.
